QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_5

question.text: What is the position limit for a client in USDINR futures and options combined?

answer.text: Higher of 6% of total open interest or USD 20 million equivalent across all exchanges
Higher of 15% of total open interest or USD 100 million equivalent
Higher of 6% of total open interest or USD 50 million equivalent
USD 10 million per exchange with no open interest linkage

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Client-level position limits for USDINR: higher of 6% of total open interest or USD 20 million equivalent across all exchanges combined. For trading members: higher of 15% of total open interest or USD 100 million. Bank TMs authorized by RBI have separate higher limits. **The Trap:** USD 100 million is the TM limit (with RBI authorization), not the client limit. USD 50 million is the TM limit for non-INR pairs (EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch7 covers currency position limits in detail. Series XIII compares these with equity position limits (Series VIII) which use MWPL as the key metric. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Users intending to take positions beyond USD 100 million must provide a written declaration to the stock exchange of the underlying exposure justifying the position.
